# @platform/contracts-admin

Tenant administration contract package: members, features, auth-settings, and per-tenant authentication provider config schemas shared by the BFF and React control plane.

## Package identity

```text
Name: @platform/contracts-admin
Version: 0.1.0
Component: contracts-admin
Type: api
System: enterprise-react-platform
Domain: core
Bounded context: tenant-administration
Owner: team-platform
```

## Lifecycle

```text
Stage: experimental
Role: contract
Class: experimental.contract
Catalog lifecycle: experimental
Visibility: internal
Support level: experimental
Review cadence: on-change
```

## Ownership

```text
Owner: team-platform
Change control: owner-review
Promotion eligible: false
Semver policy: internal-traceable
```

## Responsibilities

- Own member management schemas: MemberSummary, MemberListResponse, InviteMemberRequest, UpdateMemberRoleRequest
- Own feature-flag schemas: FeatureSummary, FeatureListResponse, ToggleFeatureRequest
- Own auth-settings read shapes the admin UI consumes: IdpSummary, MfaPolicy, SessionPolicy
- Own per-tenant authentication provider config schemas: TenantAuthProvidersConfig and update/response shapes (ADR-0037)

## Non-responsibilities

- Does not implement member, feature, or auth-settings persistence or Keycloak access
- Does not import React or any framework-specific packages
- Does not own session, permission resolution, or audit logic
- Does not import @platform/* packages

## Public exports and usage

```text
Public exports only: true
Deep imports allowed: false
```

- Imported by application and platform packages that need admin contract types
- Used at the BFF boundary to validate admin mutation bodies and by the React control plane to type its REST clients

### Allowed consumers

```text
application
platform
adapter
```

### Forbidden consumers

```text
domain
ui
```


### Consumes APIs

```text
None
```

## Operational notes

- All schemas use Zod for runtime validation
- Zero @platform/* dependencies — this is a pure contract package
- Role and provider-id literals must stay in lock-step with @platform/domain-identity and the BFF auth-providers module
